chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يَلْبَسُ الْمُحْرِمُ مِنَ الثِّيَابِ وَ مَا يُكْرَهُ لَهُ لِبَاسُهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 83 – What the one in Ihraam can wear, from the clothes, and what is disliked for him, his clothing

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/282)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/282)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْمُحْرِمِ يَلْبَسُ الْخَزَّ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Hammad Bin Usman, from Abdul Rahman Bin Al Hajjaj who said,
‘I asked Abu Al-Hassanasws about the one in Ihraam wearing Al-Khazz (animal fur). Heasws said: ‘There is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 12. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Ali from Ahmad ibn ‘A’idh from al-Husayn ibn al-Mukhtar who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, if one in the state of Ihram can wear black cloths. He (the Imam) said, ‘One cannot assume the state of Ihram in black cloths or use it for shrouding therewith . ‘“
